incidentSessions.title: DNS Name Resolution Outage
incidentSessions.symptoms: Users cannot access internet sites or internal web portals. Pinging IP addresses works, but pinging hostnames fails.
incidentSessions.expectedRca: The DNS Server service on the Domain Controller crashed because of a corrupted zone file journal (.dns.jnl) during a dynamic update stream.

alerts.message: DNS Server Service (dns.exe) is Stopped on DC-01
Name resolution lookup timeouts detected on subnet 10.10.10.0/24

logs: [2026-06-01 16:42:15] [EVENT_VIEWER] [SYSTEM] ERROR: DNS Server service terminated unexpectedly. Event ID 7034.
[2026-06-01 16:42:20] [EVENT_VIEWER] [DNS] ERROR: DNS server was unable to parse database journal for zone 'guruai.local'. Reading file 'guruai.local.dns.jnl' failed at line 1402.

timeline.description: Incident reported: DNS Name Resolution Outage

chatHistory.text: Hi Aarav, we are experiencing a critical outage with "DNS Name Resolution Outage". Clients are reporting errors and it's affecting our core service level. Can you look into this immediately?

incidentSessions.title: GPO Corrupted Sysvol Outage
incidentSessions.symptoms: Workstations are failing to apply domain security baselines. Drive maps, desktop wallpapers, and proxy configurations are missing. Windows event log shows SYSVOL path errors.
incidentSessions.expectedRca: A dynamic file replication system (DFSR) collision corrupted the GPT.INI file in the Sysvol folder structure of the primary Domain Controller, breaking group policy validation.

alerts.message: GPO SYSVOL Version Mismatch detected across DCs

logs: [2026-06-01 16:20:00] [DFSR] ERROR: DFSR failed to replicate SYSVOL due to shared access violation or file corruption on path \\guruai.local\sysvol\guruai.local\Policies\{31B2F340-016D-11D2-945F-00C04FB984F9}\gpt.ini.
[2026-06-01 16:21:40] [CLIENT_GPO] EventID 1058: Windows cannot access the file gpt.ini for GPO. Access is denied or corrupt.

timeline.description: Incident reported: GPO Corrupted Sysvol Outage

chatHistory.text: Hi Aarav, we are experiencing a critical outage with "GPO Corrupted Sysvol Outage". Clients are reporting errors and it's affecting our core service level. Can you look into this immediately?
